speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: This battle fares like to the morning’s war,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: When dying clouds contend with growing light,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What time the shepherd, blowing of his nails,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Can neither call it perfect day nor night.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Now sways it this way, like a mighty sea

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Forced by the tide to combat with the wind;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Now sways it that way, like the selfsame sea

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Forced to retire by fury of the wind.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Sometime the flood prevails, and then the wind;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Now one the better, then another best,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Both tugging to be victors, breast to breast,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Yet neither conqueror nor conquerèd.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So is the equal poise of this fell war.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Here on this molehill will I sit me down.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To whom God will, there be the victory;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For Margaret my queen and Clifford too

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Have chid me from the battle, swearing both

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: They prosper best of all when I am thence.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Would I were dead, if God’s good will were so,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For what is in this world but grief and woe?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O God! Methinks it were a happy life

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To be no better than a homely swain,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To sit upon a hill as I do now,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To carve out dials quaintly, point by point,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thereby to see the minutes how they run:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How many makes the hour full complete,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How many hours brings about the day,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How many days will finish up the year,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How many years a mortal man may live.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: When this is known, then to divide the times:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So many hours must I tend my flock,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So many hours must I take my rest,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So many hours must I contemplate,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So many hours must I sport myself,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So many days my ewes have been with young,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So many weeks ere the poor fools will ean,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So many years ere I shall shear the fleece;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So minutes, hours, days, months, and years,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Passed over to the end they were created,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Would bring white hairs unto a quiet grave.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ah, what a life were this! How sweet, how lovely!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Gives not the hawthorn bush a sweeter shade

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To shepherds looking on their silly sheep

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Than doth a rich embroidered canopy

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To kings that fear their subjects’ treachery?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O yes, it doth, a thousandfold it doth.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And to conclude, the shepherd’s homely curds,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: His cold thin drink out of his leather bottle,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: His wonted sleep under a fresh tree’s shade,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: All which secure and sweetly he enjoys,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Is far beyond a prince’s delicates—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: His viands sparkling in a golden cup,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: His body couchèd in a curious bed—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: When care, mistrust, and treason waits on him.

speaker: SON
text: Ill blows the wind that profits nobody.

speaker: SON
text: This man, whom hand to hand I slew in fight,

speaker: SON
text: May be possessèd with some store of crowns,

speaker: SON
text: And I, that haply take them from him now,

speaker: SON
text: May yet ere night yield both my life and them

speaker: SON
text: To some man else, as this dead man doth me.

speaker: SON
text: Who’s this? O God! It is my father’s face,

speaker: SON
text: Whom in this conflict I unwares have killed.

speaker: SON
text: O heavy times, begetting such events!

speaker: SON
text: From London by the King was I pressed forth.

speaker: SON
text: My father, being the Earl of Warwick’s man,

speaker: SON
text: Came on the part of York, pressed by his master.

speaker: SON
text: And I, who at his hands received my life,

speaker: SON
text: Have by my hands of life bereavèd him.

speaker: SON
text: Pardon me, God, I knew not what I did;

speaker: SON
text: And pardon, father, for I knew not thee.

speaker: SON
text: My tears shall wipe away these bloody marks,

speaker: SON
text: And no more words till they have flowed their fill.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O piteous spectacle! O bloody times!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whiles lions war and battle for their dens,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Poor harmless lambs abide their enmity.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Weep, wretched man. I’ll aid thee tear for tear,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And let our hearts and eyes, like civil war,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Be blind with tears and break, o’ercharged with grief.

speaker: FATHER
text: Thou that so stoutly hath resisted me,

speaker: FATHER
text: Give me thy gold, if thou hast any gold,

speaker: FATHER
text: For I have bought it with an hundred blows.

speaker: FATHER
text: But let me see: is this our foeman’s face?

speaker: FATHER
text: Ah, no, no, no, it is mine only son!

speaker: FATHER
text: Ah, boy, if any life be left in thee,

speaker: FATHER
text: Throw up thine eye! See, see, what showers arise,

speaker: FATHER
text: Blown with the windy tempest of my heart

speaker: FATHER
text: Upon thy wounds, that kills mine eye and heart!

speaker: FATHER
text: O, pity God this miserable age!

speaker: FATHER
text: What stratagems, how fell, how butcherly,

speaker: FATHER
text: Erroneous, mutinous, and unnatural

speaker: FATHER
text: This deadly quarrel daily doth beget!

speaker: FATHER
text: O, boy, thy father gave thee life too soon,

speaker: FATHER
text: And hath bereft thee of thy life too late!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Woe above woe, grief more than common grief!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, that my death would stay these ruthful deeds!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O pity, pity, gentle heaven, pity!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The red rose and the white are on his face,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The fatal colors of our striving houses;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The one his purple blood right well resembles,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The other his pale cheeks methinks presenteth.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Wither one rose and let the other flourish;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If you contend, a thousand lives must wither.

speaker: SON
text: How will my mother for a father’s death

speaker: SON
text: Take on with me and ne’er be satisfied!

speaker: FATHER
text: How will my wife for slaughter of my son

speaker: FATHER
text: Shed seas of tears and ne’er be satisfied!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How will the country for these woeful chances

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Misthink the King and not be satisfied!

speaker: SON
text: Was ever son so rued a father’s death?

speaker: FATHER
text: Was ever father so bemoaned his son?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Was ever king so grieved for subjects’ woe?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Much is your sorrow, mine ten times so much.

speaker: SON
text: I’ll bear thee hence, where I may weep my fill.

speaker: FATHER
text: These arms of mine shall be thy winding-sheet;

speaker: FATHER
text: My heart, sweet boy, shall be thy sepulcher,

speaker: FATHER
text: For from my heart thine image ne’er shall go.

speaker: FATHER
text: My sighing breast shall be thy funeral bell;

speaker: FATHER
text: And so obsequious will thy father be

speaker: FATHER
text: E’en for the loss of thee, having no more,

speaker: FATHER
text: As Priam was for all his valiant sons.

speaker: FATHER
text: I’ll bear thee hence, and let them fight that will,

speaker: FATHER
text: For I have murdered where I should not kill.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Sad-hearted men, much overgone with care,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Here sits a king more woeful than you are.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: Fly, father, fly, for all your friends are fled,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: And Warwick rages like a chafèd bull.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: Away, for Death doth hold us in pursuit.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Mount you, my lord; towards Berwick post amain.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Edward and Richard, like a brace of greyhounds

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Having the fearful flying hare in sight,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: With fiery eyes sparkling for very wrath

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And bloody steel grasped in their ireful hands,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Are at our backs, and therefore hence amain.

speaker: EXETER
text: Away, for Vengeance comes along with them.

speaker: EXETER
text: Nay, stay not to expostulate, make speed;

speaker: EXETER
text: Or else come after; I’ll away before.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Nay, take me with thee, good sweet Exeter;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Not that I fear to stay, but love to go

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whither the Queen intends. Forward, away!
